etymology.description: Depicts a fetus (巳) in the womb (勹) or a swaddled baby
etymology.interpretation: The character 包(bāo, 'wrap') visually represents a fetus enclosed in a womb, suggesting the idea of something being wrapped or enclosed. This portrays the concept of an item wrapped in a protective covering, just as a fetus is in the womb.

examples.chinese: 妈妈正在包饺子(媽媽正在包餃子)。
请帮我包好这个礼物(請幫我包好這個禮物)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ bāo hǎo zhège lǐwù.
examples.english: Please help me wrap this gift properly.

examples.chinese: 在中国新年，我们会包红包(在中國新年，我們會包紅包)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Zhōngguó xīnnián, wǒmen huì bāo hóngbāo.
examples.english: During the Chinese New Year, we wrap red envelopes.
